title: Find a missing side length when given area
description: Practice finding a missing side length on a rectangle when given the other side length and the area.
topic_path: Get ready for area and perimeter
Find a missing side length when given area

assessment_items.question_content:
This picture has an area of $36$ square centimeters.  The height of the picture is $6$ centimeters.

[[☃ image 2]]

**What is the width of the picture?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints:
We have a square with an area of $36$ square centimeters that has a height of $6$ centimeters.  

[[☃ image 1]]
$\text{width} = 6 \text{ centimeters}$

options.alt: A curved bracket across the left side of the rectangle is labeled 6 centimeters. A curved bracket across the bottom side of the rectangle is labeled width.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangle has an area of $32$ square millimeters.  The length of the rectangle is $8$ millimeters.

**What is the width of the rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] millimeters
hints:
We need to create a rectangle out of $32$ square millimeters that has a length of $8$ millimeters.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width}$

$32= 8\times \text{width}$

We can divide to find the width.

$32\div 8= \text{width}$
$\text{width} = 4\text{ millimeters}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangle has an area of $15$ square millimeters.  The width of the rectangle is $3$ millimeters.

**What is the length of the rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] millimeters
hints:
We need to create a rectangle out of $15$ square millimeters that has a width of $3$ millimeters.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width}$

$15=  \text{length} \times 3$

We can divide to find the length.

$15\div 3= \text{length}$
$\text{length} = 5\text{ millimeters}$

$24=  \text{length} \times 4$

We can divide to find the length.

$24\div 4= \text{length}$
$\text{length} = 6\text{ meters}$
